{{Blurimage |caption=Photograph of '''Level 107''' during winter. |file=Better imagers.png}} {{Glacier Theme}} ==Description== A sea of distinctive, yet repetitive, cityscape expanding up to some 4,000 kilometers, forming the only place most inhabitants and passersby will ever see, blanketed by a near-continual state of snowfall, is what forms Level 107 (Xaorézhǚ). The expansive buildings conforming to most of Xaorézhǚ's livable portions are abandoned but remain adorned and well-kept, upholding a subtle balance of symmetry and formality amidst the convoluted, changing architectural styles distributed by region and building types. Soviet architecture compounds a significant proportion of preexisting constructions. Away from the center, the mighty structures fall into a state of dilapidation or permanent construction, contrasted by dwarfed huts and houses. Beyond urban hell, the level witnesses a sharp decline in the protruding structure's uniformity, size, modernity, and appearance. Primitive housing traditionally seen in nomadic times is hidden in between the waning greenery and natural curvature. Silence permeates these far reaches, and the stars of the boundless upper horizons are seen in their utmost glory; ponds or rivers are solemnly seen and serve as heated homes for plants and sealife. The environment further out from the outskirts is deeply divergent from the land a few kilometers away; the Flatlands, as it became known, is devoid of all life. An expanse unbounded and covered in thin lines of snow atop a concrete foundation, whose monotonously indifferent environment is at times broken by near-pristine helicopters that offer supplies and military equipmentIt is arguably among the sole reasons Aurielle continues to explore the region. . The sky remains a constant ebony with no distinct celestial objects; it is not advised to stay for long. {{Blurimage |caption=The outskirts of the city. |file=Minus 213.png |align=left}} Industrial behemoths scarcely litter the outskirts, marking a clear distinction between the mechanical and primitivist. Their insides are unusable outside of metal gathering, as they are nonsensical and sprawl into incomprehensible patterns, migrating from place to place at every say-so—whether they are alive or not is heavily debated. Emulated sounds of churning metal and distorted humming sounds with discernible patterns, although at times stagnant and abrupt, are emitted indefinitely by the machines. Imitations and familiar sounds, such as those of Suburbia's Radio Field, are shrieked by the beasts in unpurposeful intervals. In the dead center of the level lies an enormously tall building known as "Dem Edifeceo."[[Aurielleno]] for The Edifice. Housing the epicenter of Auriellen influence to both Cascada and the level, the massive edifice is operated by Rama Saom. Its interior is nonsensical, as if it had no inherent identity and instead coalesced all into one facadic amalgamation. All its faces have no gradual transition into one another, instead abruptly changing thoughts of what it wanted to be. From libraries to research facilities and greenhouses to balconies observing their surroundings like the inept seeing if they are judged, they all comprise the building. Stores and commercial structures attached to the many pillars offer different commodities that ramify based on need. The epitome of survivalism, currency, specifically the Cascadan Zhou,The basic monetary unit of the Duchy of Cascada, there are many stores within the level that offer Zhou's in exchange for [[Object List|objects]]. is used to carry out exchanges. Xaorézhǚ has long been tainted by civilization, cracks of which etch themselves through cultural traditions but most deeply within the scabs left after conflict. {{Blurimage |caption=Helicopters, found in the flatland during a Cascadan-Auriellen expedition. |file=Agent in your team is down.png}} The frigid air of the level wanes in seasonal patterns. Each season is equal in length, but its temperatures vary wildly and have greatly influenced the practices done by the local population. Dismissive of season, snow sweeps the environment incessantly through the day, only dissipating as it falters. Clouds congregate in tandem with snowfall, yet vanish with it, or in spring times. The sky in the city and outskirts is dark and full of [[Phenomenon 8|stars]]; their departure brings forth the dissipation of all snow. ==Entities== Level 107's hostile entity population is limited. [[Entity 8|Hounds]] and [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] are only located in the flatlands, though sightings have decreased dramatically over the years due to AEG-Cascadan operations. Cascadans, however, are the most prevelant entity within it. {{LinkCollapse |num=furries |initial= + Rama Saom File - "Cascadans" |final= - Rama Saom File - "Cascadans" |body=
[[File:Rama Saom.png|link=|center|300px]]
Cascadans, also nicknamed Furcoats or Furballs, are the native inhabitants of Level 107. They are a branch of the Homo sapiens sapiens that, both by adapting to the frigid environment of the level and a now-eradicated virus, exhibit notable animal charasteristics such as furs, snouths, and tails, among other adaptions,However, Cascadans do not have whiskers. while retaining various human aspects such as bipedality and intelligence. Cascadans act and interact near-identically to us, treating wanderers as if they were the same (albeit with more respect due to the cultural etiquette of the Duchy of Cascada and views of wanderers as 'visitors'). They can communicate with one another using an intricate language known as "[[Jinyi]]" and, as shown by the Duchy of Cascada, can form civilizations the same as us. Cascadans, like their animal counterparts, are natural hunters and possess a heightened sense of awareness, hearing, and sight. Cascadans usually represent animals that differ depending on the instance, though they are most commonly animals from the Felidae and Canidae families. Additionally, most Cascadans have purely white fur, with exceptions for species (such as snow leopards) that naturally live in cold regions. Following are 3 sketches. Cascadan fellars.png|Sketch depicts three CSOC operators. Profile Thing.png|Sketch depicts the events that led to the Cascadan Conflict. File:Uvab profile.png|Sketch depicting an Auriellen and Cascadan in a firefight.
}} ==Settlements== The [[Duchy of Cascada]] exercises joint control and supervision of the level with [[Republic of Aurielle]]. {{LinkCollapse |num=verifyyourclocks |initial= + ASCIC Factbook - 'Duchy of Cascada' |final= - ASCIC Factbook - 'Duchy of Cascada' |body=
[[File:Assembleu di Securitate Aueielleno Centreo Istisnem.png|center|400px]] {{Simple Image Block |image=Duxeam di Cascadea.png |caption=Flag of Cascada.}} Cascada, officially the Duchy of Cascada, ([[Aurielleno]]: Duxeam di Cascadea, [[Jinyi]]: Kǎsùdea Kōnguó), is a client and semi-vassal state governed by the Republic of Aurielle that comprises most of the city in Level 107, certain areas of the outskirts, and recently, per legislations allowing exploration to levels beyond their homeland, [[Level 330.1]] alongside other cold levels. Piqenq serves as Cascada's capital, although Nomkh acts as the group's largest economic hub. The government divides Cascada into 22 provinces and 43 subsidiary areas. Approximately 46,000 Cascadans and 4,000 Humans live in Cascada.
Military
The Duchy of Cascada primarily exports valuables such as weapons attachments, weaponry, mawfishA special type of fish only found in Level 107 that is used widely in Auriellen and Cascadan MREs for their nutrition., and ammunition. Much of the Cascadan military uses equipment directly imported, purchased, or gifted from Aurielle. Despite their apparent dependence on Aurielle, their domestic arms manufacturing and military hardware are known for being affordable and reliable.
Politics
Aurielle influenced Cascada to become a unitary parliamentary constitutional monarchy, where the duke serves primarily as a ceremonial monarch, while the prime minister acts as the elected head of government. The bicameral parliament, known as the National Assembly, serves as the legislative organ. It consists of a lower House of Representatives and an upper Palace of Councillors. The government grants universal suffrage to all citizens 19 and over.
Culture
In Cascadan society, familial cohesion and respect for seniors have been paramount for much of its existence. Cascadan etiquette is strict, both regarding work and in regular scenarios. Elders and individuals of higher social status must be followed and respected, and anyone below them must consider themselves as 'inferior.' Regular Cascadans are nice and selfless due to these societal norms. Although, luckily, the work culture of Cascada is considerably tamer than its Frontrooms equivalents, with individual rights being reserved. The Duchy faces a prevalent issue of gender inequality. Men statistically receive higher-end jobs and more opportunities for upper positions, and domestic abuse and objectification of women previously occurred quite commonly. However, both Aurielle's pressure and protests to end gender inequality have led to a steady decline of these practices.
}} Aside from the Duchy of Cascada, there are various settlements, most from Aurielle. ===Hacerom Lesath=== Hacerom Lesath is the main headquarters of Rama Saom and its various divisions located in Dem Edificeo. It houses a total of 1,495 people. Interactions with the settlement are scarce due to the area being restricted to Rama Saom and Auriellen personnel, though they are typically positive and show willingness by guardsmen to assist wanderers. ===Devon=== The largest Auriellen settlement within the level with a population of 459. ===Waypoint Emissary=== With a fixed population of 45 operatives, Waypoint Emissary is an embassy created by the AEG under the Devon Agreement. It serves as the center and symbol of AEG-Cascadan cooperation and trade post-Cascadan Conflict. {{Blurimage |file=Tabu.png |caption=Entrance to Level 107.1 in Cheojin}} ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Under certain conditions, in [[Level 213]] during Freeze Dining, a cold metal door may appear that will lead here. Upon entering, one will find themself outside of a store called 'ID'; *Secluded areas in [[Level 82]] eventually morph into this level. Wanderers usually find themselves in alleys; *Peculiarly cold areas within [[Level 330]]'s Empty Mall will lead here. ===Exits=== *Entering a hotel called "Sonne" will take wanderers to [[Level 330.1]]; *Deeper areas of HuidongA Cascadan city/settlement within the level. have undergrounds entrances that lead to [[The Delta Mall]]; *Rarely, no-clipping in bowling alleys within the level will lead to [[Level 499]]; *Stairs in Cheojin, Jarkim, Kiangke, and various other cities in Level 107, Cascada with the label "Tabù Cistern" will led to [[Level 107.1]]; *Noclipping through heavy-duty electrical equipment sends one to [[Level 169]]. ---- {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|num=238232|initial='''> Open Author and Licensing Credits'''|final='''> Close Author and Licensing Credits'''|body= Author: [[User:Dulling|Dulling]]
